title: Loop Spec
description: Machine-checkable specification of an agentic loop. Encodes the loop-design skill's non-negotiables: a four-part goal contract, a scope, a verifier with a named trust rung, on-disk state, and three stop conditions (success, hard cap, no-progress). Structural rules live here; judgment rules (e.g. self-grading is off the ladder) live in validate_loop_spec.py.

goal.description: The goal as a contract, not a prompt. All four parts must be explicit; a vague part gets filled with the easiest reading.

end_state.description: The objective pass condition. Must be true/false checkable, e.g. 'tests in /auth pass'.

evidence.description: What proves the end state was reached, e.g. 'pytest exits 0'. Should map to the verifier.

constraints.description: What the agent must not break getting there. May be empty array, but must be stated deliberately.

budget.description: Human summary of the work it may spend (the machine-enforced version lives in stop_conditions).

may_touch.description: Paths/resources the loop may read or edit.

loop_shape.description: completion = run until a checkable end state is true (/goal-style). cadence = re-run on a schedule for open-ended work (/loop, cron; the PR-babysitter shape).

unattended.description: True if it runs without a human present. Raises the bar on verification.

iteration.description: The steps performed each pass (the EACH ITERATION list). First step typically loads state from disk; a retry step should change approach, not repeat.

rung.description: Verifier ladder rung. tool = computational gate (strongest). independent_model = different model judging the artifact. human = human gate (=> not autonomous). self = maker grades itself (OFF the ladder; flagged as error by the validator).

check.description: The concrete gate, e.g. 'schema valid AND row_count > 0'.

independent.description: True if the checker is separate from the maker and not given the maker's justification.

end_to_end.description: True if the check exercises the real system (live endpoint, browser, simulator) rather than a self-reported unit result. Strongly recommended for unattended loops.

architecture.description: fresh_restart = clean context each pass, state reloaded from disk (decomposable work). compaction = one session, summarize-and-continue near the limit (continuous thread).

on_disk.description: Must be true. Durable state in the conversation is the context-accumulation anti-pattern.

durable_context.description: Files holding conventions/rules the agent re-reads every run, e.g. ['CLAUDE.md'].

success.description: The success exit (usually equals goal.end_state / verifier.check).

budget.description: At least one resource cap. Prevents metered burn.

no_progress.description: Stall detection. Halt when a no-progress signal repeats.

repeats.description: Halt after this many consecutive occurrences.

on_stop.description: What to do on any stop: summarize changes + open gaps; must never claim a false 'done'.

destination.description: Where the result/summary goes, e.g. 'Slack #eng-bots', 'PR comment', 'page on-call'.

execution.description: Concurrency and isolation. Relevant once more than one agent runs.

isolation.description: Per-agent private checkout. Required once parallelism > 1 to avoid parallel clobber.

proven_cheap.description: True if this loop has demonstrated an acceptable cost per accepted change.

autonomy.description: How much the loop may run without a human. The MAX safe level is computed by the validator from the gate rung, scope fence, budget, reversibility, and a proven manual pass; 'requested' must not exceed it.

requested.description: L0 advise/dry-run; L1 propose & confirm at each decision point; L2 act with guardrails (pause only at irreversible/budget/no-progress); L3 unattended (trigger, no human, report after).

output_reversibility.description: reversible = a PR/draft/revertable change; outward_facing = posts/sends/publishes; irreversible = prod write, payment, deletion. Anything but 'reversible' caps autonomy below L3.

proven_manual_pass.description: True only after at least one watched/manual run has completed successfully with the intended gate. Required for L3; separate from economics.proven_cheap.

meta.description: Open extension point for harness-specific fields.

allOf.description: A completion loop has a single finish line, so stop_conditions.success is required. A cadence loop runs open-ended, so it isn't.
